author: @martinwinfield2935
text: Just would like to ask, how do you change the battery with the clock glued in?

author: @davidhilton7780
text: Strait forward, to the point, I like it!

author: @NorthernKitty
text:
Great job!!  Looks very nice!!
Of course, you know what your NEXT project will have to be... making a broken clock into a working hard drive!!  😋  (Wouldn't that be a trick?)

author: @hubrisnaut
text: Great concept. I really like that. Techies would go crazy over those as a product. Why "throw those bits in the bin"? (I'm in America so I assume that means recycling or trash.)

author: @TrollingAround
text: Use the motor as a generator.

author: @theGreaterAwareness
text: Hello Luke! With that being said, it does bring me to the end of my comment.

author: @timsuttonlovinlife-
text: Awesome 👏, The enthusiasm in what you’re doing is inspiring to watch, love it

author: @tawnihaynie1065
text: Hi Robert and Luke.  Great video.  Please stay safe and healthy and take care of yourself and your family members.  To all members also.  ☺☺☺😇😇😇

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you very much! Stay safe! :)

author: @Teknopottu
text: Nice video and good idea for a gift to someone appreciating reusing stuff/mechanical art!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Cheers mate! I'm debating if I should gift it or keep it on my desk? We will see lol. :)

author: @rastamanralph6670
text: If you throw your copper in the bin you can post it to me and I will pay for the postage and I will recycle it.😉👍

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahaha i regret it LOL. I might have to go save that poor copper hahah. Cheers mate! :)

replies.author: @rastamanralph6670
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey copper is a precious metal and will soon be worth more than cash. Look on eBay at the price old copper 1p coins go for.👊👍

author: @AM-us7cz
text: Gday Luke, I was going to skip this video but I didn't and I'm pleased I didn't. Thanks mate 👍

author: @darthfate2139
text: Nice clock, but if you have next time an idea to cut that disc plate with angle grinder plz dont do it or do it outside. You never know what material is disc plate. I tried this and had serious breathing problems. That dust is nothing good..

replies.author: @cptrikester2671
replies.text: I think the inaccessible battery has been glued onto the clock.

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Add 4 lines for 12, 3, 6, 9 o'clock and Bob's your auntie.
By the way, when you said throw the coil in the bin my heart skipped a beat, are you kidding me. That goes in a coils drawer.

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
Bin as in parts bin.
But they probably have a coil bin as well.

author: @czechyorker
text: Is that magnet back in place? isn't it going to "slow" the clock?

replies.author: @fuller-media
replies.text: thats the flux capacitor - thanks luke Im building one - how do you set destination year?

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: I don't think the magnet went back in.

replies.author: @fuller-media
replies.text: @stevetobias4890 Great news, I replicated Lukes time machine and came back from todays vacation yesterday - the magnet fluxing across to the platen works!

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @fuller-media lmao, can you tell me what tomorrow's lotto numbers will be?

replies.author: @fuller-media
replies.text: @stevetobias4890 Yes, except greed prevents that share - might I sugest the construction of a Chronovisor
